---
title: "About"
format: html
---

## About this course

This course provides an introduction to key concepts in statistics and data science, with an emphasis on **understanding, interpretation, and communication** rather than rote computation.

Students will engage with real and realistic datasets, develop intuition through visualization and simulation, and learn to clearly communicate their findings.


## Teaching philosophy

This course is designed around the following principles:

- **Conceptual understanding first**  
  We focus on *why* methods work and *how to interpret results*, not just how to compute them.

- **Reproducibility matters**  
  All work should be clear, organized, and reproducible using modern tools.

- **Learning by doing**  
  Students will practice through labs, assignments, and in-class activities.

- **Communication is key**  
  Being able to explain results clearly is just as important as obtaining them.


## Course structure

The course typically includes:

- **Lectures**  
  Introduce key concepts and worked examples

- **Labs / practice**  
  Reinforce ideas through hands-on work

- **Assignments**  
  Apply methods to new problems and datasets

- **Assessments**  
  Evaluate conceptual understanding and applied skills


## Tools and technologies

This course uses:

- **R and RStudio / Positron**
- **Quarto** for reproducible documents and presentations
- **WebR** (in some cases) for running code in the browser
- **GitHub** for version control and collaboration (optional/advanced)

No prior experience with these tools is assumed unless otherwise stated.


## Expectations

Students are expected to:

- attend and participate in lectures
- engage actively with course materials
- attempt all assigned work
- ask questions and seek help when needed
- collaborate appropriately while maintaining academic integrity


## Support

Learning new concepts can be challenging. Support is available through:

- office hours
- discussion forums
- peer collaboration (where appropriate)

If you are struggling, please reach out early.


## A note on flexibility

The course schedule and materials may be adjusted based on pacing and feedback to best support student learning.
